title: prostanthera arapilensis
intro: Prostanthera arapilensis, also known as Arapiles mint-bush, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is native to Victoria, Australia, and is a shrub that is typically 0.2-1.5 meters in height. The plant has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. Prostanthera arapilensis can be prepared as a tea or infused oil, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
